medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient with a history of cervical and lumbar stenosis with cervical spondylosis and myelopathy s/p recent spinal surgery presents with delirium/confusion x several weeks, here with agitation from her skilled nursing facility; she has been uncooperative with care and has been refusing physical therapy. Family reports that she never regained her baseline mental status since L3-5 laminectomy, L3-5 posterior lumbar fusion surgery. In the ED, pt has EKG showing NSR at 93, LAD, no ischemic changes. CBC, chem 7, coags, serum tox, UA, uring tox, CXR, CT head were all performed without any clear identifiable cause. Patient had increasing agitation and delirium.
medical_history.past_medical_history: 1. HTN 2. OA 3. diet controlled DM 4. OSA on BiPAP 5. GERD 6. Lumbar Spinal Stenosis 7. L sided Sciatica 8. Osteoporosis 9. Obesity 10. Narrow angle glaucoma 11. Allergic rhinitis 12. Fibroid uterus 13. hyperlipidemia. Surgical history includes L3-5 laminectomy and L3-5 posterior lumbar fusion.

physical_examination.general_examination: VS on transfer: 98.5°F, 96/42 BP, HR 105, RR 18, 100% RA. A&O x2. Patient resting comfortably though agitated at times.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: uncooperative with exam, pupils equal and reactive. MSE: word salad, occasional neologisms, and word finding difficulty.
